ogTitle: Maidenwell vs Main Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Maidenwell and Main Beach.
heading: Maidenwell vs Main Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Maidenwell, Queensland and Main Beach, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Main Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Maidenwell (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Maidenwell or Main Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Maidenwell has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Main Beach scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Maidenwell scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Main Beach scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Maidenwell or Main Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Main Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
